steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the whole passage. The passage has two parts joined by "But": Nixon is most famous for the Watergate scandal, but his legacy also includes real achievements. The main idea must capture both sides of that contrast, not just one detail from either side.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Chuck Dawson and John Reed were significant figures in the presidency of Richard Nixon.: Dawson and Reed are mentioned only as examples of Nixon's associates in the scandal — they are supporting detail, not the main point. The passage is about Nixon, not them. ✗
  • Choice B — Richard Nixon is commonly linked with an infamous historical event, but this overshadows some of his notable achievements.: This captures the "But" structure exactly: public reputation (Watergate) vs. overlooked achievements (EPA, healthcare). ✓
  • Choice C — Some of Richard Nixon's policies influenced the policies of later presidential administrations.: A true supporting detail — the ACA connection is mentioned — but this is only one piece of his achievements and ignores the Watergate side entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — Richard Nixon's reputation is primarily due to the actions of his associates.: Contradicts the passage, which says Nixon had his own "participation" in Watergate. Associates are mentioned as part of the scandal, not as its cause. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
